title: Connector Startup and Initialization Flow
interaction_flow: KafkaConnectWorker → BinlogConnector: start(Map<String,String> props)
BinlogConnector → BinlogConnector: stores @Immutable Map<String,String> properties
KafkaConnectWorker → BinlogConnector: validate(config) [Kafka Connect validation phase]
BinlogConnector → BinlogConnectorConfig: createConnectorConfig(Configuration config)
BinlogConnectorConfig → CommonConnectorConfig: super(config) — resolves maxQueueSize, maxBatchSize, pollInterval, heartbeatInterval, snapshotMode, logicalName
BinlogConnector → BinlogConnectorConnection: createConnection(Configuration config, T connectorConfig)
BinlogConnector → BinlogConnector: validateConnection(Map<String,ConfigValue> configValues, Configuration config)
BinlogConnectorConnection → DB: test JDBC connectivity (SHOW VARIABLES, SHOW MASTER STATUS)
KafkaConnectWorker → BinlogConnector: taskConfigs(int maxTasks)
BinlogConnector → KafkaConnectWorker: returns List<Map<String,String>> (one config map per task)
KafkaConnectWorker → BinlogSourceTask: instantiate + start(props)
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogConnectorConfig: createConnectorConfig(config) — parse hostName, port, snapshotMode, connectionTimeout, GTID settings
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogConnectorConnection: createConnection(config, connectorConfig)
BinlogConnectorConnection → DB: readSystemVariables() — SHOW VARIABLES
BinlogConnectorConnection → DB: readCharsetSystemVariables()
BinlogConnectorConnection → DB: isBinlogFormatRow() — verify ROW format
BinlogConnectorConnection → DB: isBinlogRowImageFull() — verify FULL image
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogOffsetContext.Loader: load stored offsets from Kafka Connect offset storage
BinlogOffsetContext.Loader → BinlogOffsetContext: reconstruct(restartBinlogFilename, restartBinlogPosition, restartGtidSet, eventsToSkip, rowsToSkip)
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogConnectorConnection: validateLogPosition(partition, offsetContext, config)
BinlogConnectorConnection → DB: isBinlogPositionAvailable(config, gtid, binlogFileName) — check offset still valid
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogSourceTask: validateSnapshotFeasibility(snapshotter, offsetContext, connection)
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogSourceTask: validateBinlogConfiguration(snapshotter, connection)
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogDatabaseSchema: initialize schema registry
BinlogSourceTask → BinlogTaskContext: create(connectorConfig, connection, schema, metrics)
BinlogSourceTask → KafkaConnectWorker: task ready — begins snapshot or streaming
key_relationships: BinlogConnector creates BinlogConnectorConnection via createConnection(Configuration, T)
BinlogTaskContext holds BinlogConnectorConfig + BinlogConnectorConnection + BinlogDatabaseSchema + BinlogStreamingChangeEventSourceMetrics
BinlogOffsetContext.Loader is inner class of BinlogOffsetContext — reads Kafka Connect offset map
BinlogSourceTask contains BinlogHeartbeatErrorHandler inner class (handles SQL heartbeat errors on startup)

BinlogTaskContext: (no extracted methods — container for config, connection, schema, metrics)

config_highlights.snapshot_modes: configured via 'snapshot.mode' and 'snapshot.locking.mode' fields; supports configuration-based mode flags
config_highlights.GTID_support: BinlogConnectorConfig has GTID_SOURCE_INCLUDES/EXCLUDES Field definitions for GTID-based replication recovery

important_notes: BinlogConnector is abstract — concrete implementations are MySqlConnector and MariaDbConnector which supply createConnection() and createConnectorConfig(). The startup sequence has two phases: (1) Connector-level validation triggered by Kafka Connect's validate() → taskConfigs(); (2) Task-level initialization inside BinlogSourceTask.start() which does JDBC setup, offset loading, binlog validation, and schema init before transitioning to snapshot or streaming. BinlogConnectorConnection uses SQL queries like 'SHOW VARIABLES', 'SHOW MASTER STATUS' to verify binlog accessibility.
